title: Gate advisor-inserted optional phases on cycle class/size — coverage-gate+bug-repro+fault-loc+flake-rerun cost ~4.7M tokens/cycle and are near-pure overhead on trivial cycles
why: MEASURED (knowledge-base/research/token-usage-history-2026-07-20.md): per-run cache-read averages — coverage-gate 1.67M, bug-reproduction 1.31M, flake-rerun-scan 0.87M, fault-localization 0.83M. These advisor-inserted optionals fire liberally regardless of cycle size/class; on trivial/docs/config cycles they add ~4.7M tokens with little expected value. The integrity floor already models class-aware skipping (tdd unless trivial; cycle_size_estimate exists in cycle-state).
how_to_apply: phases_are_config_only + phase_settings_from_config_not_code: (1) extend the advisor plan clamp so optional-phase insertion consults cycle class (bugfix => bug-repro/fault-loc justified; feature/docs/trivial => skip unless the advisor JUSTIFIES with a cited signal); (2) declare per-phase insertion predicates in the phase catalog/policy (e.g. applies_to: [bugfix]) rather than Go literals; (3) integrity floor untouched — mandatory spine phases are never gated; (4) telemetry proves the delta (expect ~30-40% wrapper reduction on non-bugfix cycles). Wiring proof: a trivial-class cycle plan must show the skipped optionals and the reason in the routing plan artifact.
evidence: per-phase usage aggregation cycles>=900; advisor-inserted phase run counts (coverage-gate 40, bug-repro 36, fault-loc 47 across ~85 cycles); existing trivial-class floor precedent (tdd unless trivial).
